jokes.text: Nothing like seeing E.T. for the 64th time to pick up your day.
jokes.setup: Character mentions seeing a popular movie
jokes.punchline: The absurdly high number of viewings (64)
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment showing Jim's obsessive nature. The specific number '64' is funny and shows commitment to the bit.

jokes.text: Does this look like I'm sick of it?
jokes.setup: Alex asks if Jim is sick of E.T. after seeing it 64 times
jokes.punchline: Jim's visual response (presumably showing obsessive enthusiasm)
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor showing Jim's obsession through implied visual reaction

jokes.text: Actually, there's this kid... Fat kid, wears glasses, 13 years old... Who's just seen it for the 74th time.
jokes.setup: Someone suggests Jim has seen E.T. more than anyone
jokes.punchline: Jim knows of a specific kid who's seen it even more
jokes.explanation: Well-crafted character moment showing Jim's awareness of fellow obsessives

jokes.text: I haven't seen it.
jokes.setup: Everyone enthusiastically discussing their favorite E.T. moments
jokes.punchline: Alex admits he hasn't seen the movie everyone's obsessed with
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing and economy - simple admission creates maximum awkwardness

jokes.text: Because I don't like science fiction movies, all right?
jokes.setup: Alex being pressed repeatedly about why he hasn't seen E.T.
jokes.punchline: He finally admits he doesn't like sci-fi, dismissing the beloved film
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Alex's stubbornness against popular opinion

jokes.text: And by the way, don't sit next to a little fat kid with glasses.
jokes.setup: Jim giving Alex money to see E.T.
jokes.punchline: Callback to the specific kid he mentioned earlier
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that adds new information while referencing earlier joke

jokes.text: Lilewoowi De Palma?
jokes.setup: Drunk caller trying to ask for Louie
jokes.punchline: Mispronunciation of 'Little Louie De Palma'
jokes.explanation: Effective drunk speech pattern that reveals character information

jokes.text: Now, who was that, Lilewoowi?
jokes.setup: Iggy asking about the phone call
jokes.punchline: Using the drunk caller's mispronunciation to mock Louie
jokes.explanation: Quick callback that shows character dynamic - Iggy seizing opportunity to needle Louie

jokes.text: You mean that prison guard?
jokes.setup: Louie describing a past romantic interest with flowery language
jokes.punchline: Alex immediately deflates the romance with harsh reality
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of romantic pretension with brutal economy and character insight

jokes.text: Excuse me, for those of you interested in accuracy, I'm going to do a simultaneous translation on this one.
jokes.setup: Louie spinning romantic tale about Emily
jokes.punchline: Alex announcing he'll provide translation of Louie's delusions
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-device that sets up extended comedic bit while showing Alex's role as reality check

jokes.text: She had an unusual quality that I love in a woman. She managed to stomach him.
jokes.setup: Louie: 'She had an unusual quality that I love in a woman'
jokes.punchline: Alex: 'She managed to stomach him'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant simultaneous translation joke with perfect structure and brutal honesty

jokes.text: She fell in love with me like that. She was tanked up on pills and booze. Somebody just dumped her.
jokes.setup: Louie: 'She fell in love with me like that'
jokes.punchline: Alex: 'She was tanked up on pills and booze. Somebody just dumped her'
jokes.explanation: Continues translation bit with escalating harsh reality versus romantic delusion

jokes.text: But our love, even like the most beautiful flower, wilted and fell. She sobered up.
jokes.setup: Louie's flowery metaphor about love wilting
jokes.punchline: Alex's blunt two-word explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and structure - two words demolish an elaborate romantic metaphor

jokes.text: Didn't you say that today? I say it every day. Who hasn't?
jokes.setup: Louie complaining no one has called him gross and disgusting out loud
jokes.punchline: Multiple characters revealing they say it constantly
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing everyone's true feelings about Louie

jokes.text: Well, it's so sad that she mixed drugs and alcohol and ended up spending the night with you when most people only die.
jokes.setup: Louie asking why Alex said 'poor Emily'
jokes.punchline: Alex suggesting sleeping with Louie is worse than the usual fatal outcome of mixing drugs and alcohol
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor with perfect structure - genuinely shocking and quotable punchline

jokes.text: I'm going to go down there and sluice down that little tart until there's eye makeup running out of her shoes.
jokes.setup: Louie planning revenge by throwing a drink on Emily
jokes.punchline: Elaborate, specific description of the drink-throwing aftermath
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie character moment - elaborate, vindictive imagery delivered with relish

jokes.text: Heavily sedated, possibly suicidal?
jokes.setup: Bartender asking for description of woman Louie is meeting
jokes.punchline: Louie's matter-of-fact description emphasizes Emily's problems
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Louie's blunt assessment of Emily's state

jokes.text: Yeah, yeah, uh, uh, let me have something, uh, tall, cold, and sticky... Something that would be impossible to wash out of your hair.
jokes.setup: Bartender asking what Louie wants to drink
jokes.punchline: Louie ordering a drink specifically for throwing purposes
jokes.explanation: Good setup for revenge plan with character-specific vindictive detail

jokes.text: Hello, Emily, you little pill popper.
jokes.setup: Louie approaching Emily for his planned revenge
jokes.punchline: Greeting that immediately reveals his contempt
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate insult that sets up his planned confrontation

jokes.text: Hold that thought.
jokes.setup: Emily praising Louie as the most wonderful, remarkable man
jokes.punchline: Louie pausing her praise to throw the drink (implied)
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing - interrupting praise for revenge creates maximum comedic impact

jokes.text: Hey, Mac, you got a bar rag back there or something?
jokes.setup: Louie has just thrown drink on Emily
jokes.punchline: Casually asking for cleanup supplies
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan follow-up to the drink throwing - matter-of-fact aftermath

jokes.text: No chance, Sundance, uh-uh.
jokes.setup: Emily asking to go back to Louie's place
jokes.punchline: Louie's rhyming rejection
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay that fits Louie's character but not particularly original

jokes.text: Because it's not you talking, it's your pharmacist.
jokes.setup: Emily asking why he won't go with her
jokes.punchline: Suggesting her drugs are making the request, not her
jokes.explanation: Clever way to address Emily's drug problem. The personification of the pharmacist is witty.

jokes.text: Emily, your purse should have a childproof cap.
jokes.setup: Emily denying that pills govern her actions
jokes.punchline: Louie comparing her purse to a pill bottle
jokes.explanation: Clever metaphor that's both specific and insulting in Louie's style

jokes.text: Gross, disgusting, delightful. Right.
jokes.setup: Emily calling Louie delightful
jokes.punchline: Louie sarcastically adding it to his list of descriptors
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Louie's self-awareness and cynicism

jokes.text: ♪ It had to be you ♪
jokes.setup: Louie walking out on Emily after his revenge
jokes.punchline: Emily singing romantic song to manipulate him back
jokes.explanation: Sets up Emily's manipulation tactic - singing to draw him back

jokes.text: ♪ It had to be... ♪ ♪ Me. ♪
jokes.setup: Emily's romantic song manipulation
jokes.punchline: Louie joining in, showing he's completely fallen for it
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Louie's complete capitulation despite his revenge plans

jokes.text: You've had the handsome guys. You've had, uh, the rich guys. You've had the smooth guys. You've had the, uh, sharp guys; the, uh, smart guys; the tough guys, the young guys. Well, I'm none of those.
jokes.setup: Louie's speech about why Emily should be with him
jokes.punchline: His admission that he has none of the attractive qualities
jokes.explanation: Good character moment where Louie's self-awareness is both funny and touching. The build-up and deflation work well.

jokes.text: Look who's on first base.
jokes.setup: Emily seeing Louie covered in food/makeup
jokes.punchline: Baseball metaphor for romantic progress despite his disheveled state
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag about Louie's messy appearance. The baseball metaphor is mildly clever but not exceptional.

jokes.text: What does this tell you?
jokes.setup: Jim asking if Alex really liked E.T. or just said it for the money
jokes.punchline: Alex's implied gesture/expression showing genuine emotion
jokes.explanation: Simple visual beat showing Alex was genuinely moved. Functional moment but relies on visual performance.

jokes.text: E.T. phone home. Home phone. Phone home, E.T. Elliott. Elliott. Elliott.
jokes.setup: Alex has just seen E.T. and loved it
jokes.punchline: Immediate obsessive quoting of movie dialogue
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Alex immediately becoming as obsessed as Jim

jokes.text: She didn't like my overcoat.
jokes.setup: Alex asking why Louie is dressed inappropriately for snow
jokes.punchline: Revealing Emily controls even his winter clothing choices
jokes.explanation: Good visual setup showing extent of Emily's control over Louie

jokes.text: Look, honey, I can't get the blueberries. I looked... I looked all over for the blueberries. I-I-I went every... I went 20 different places, but... No, no. Yeah, okay.
jokes.setup: Louie on the phone with Emily
jokes.punchline: His pathetic pleading about failing to find blueberries
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe comedy. Louie's desperate apology over something as mundane as blueberries shows how whipped he's become.

jokes.text: Well, on behalf of New York City, let me say that I'm glad she liked your pants.
jokes.setup: Louie explaining Emily didn't like his overcoat
jokes.punchline: Alex sarcastically thanking Emily for approving Louie's pants
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic comment playing on the visual of Louie underdressed

jokes.text: A romantic fool. No. A weenie.
jokes.setup: Louie asking what you call a man searching for blueberries in snow
jokes.punchline: Alex's blunt correction of Louie's romanticized view
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Louie's self-image with brutal honesty

jokes.text: One of those weenies who would do anything that a broad asked him and then say, 'Yes, dear.'
jokes.setup: Louie describing the type of man he's becoming
jokes.punchline: Specific description of submissive husband behavior
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Louie's self-awareness of his transformation

jokes.text: Look at her, Reiger. She's beautiful. She's beautiful!
jokes.setup: Alex asking how Louie got into this mess
jokes.punchline: Louie pointing out Emily, presumably looking disheveled or problematic
jokes.explanation: Visual gag relying on the contrast between Louie's assessment and Emily's actual appearance. Basic but functional.

jokes.text: She's beautiful! She's beautiful!
jokes.setup: Alex asking how Louie got into this mess
jokes.punchline: Louie's desperate, repeated declaration while looking at Emily
jokes.explanation: Shows Louie's complete infatuation and loss of perspective

jokes.text: You said you love me! You said you love me.
jokes.setup: Emily explaining their dysfunctional relationship dynamics
jokes.punchline: Louie fixating only on her admission of love
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Louie missing the point entirely

jokes.text: Do-do-do... I don't think you could do it. Do-do I have to squeeze more oranges?
jokes.setup: Emily saying there's only one way she'll love him forever
jokes.punchline: Louie's pathetic eagerness to do whatever task she wants
jokes.explanation: Shows depth of Louie's servitude through specific domestic task

jokes.text: Right.
jokes.setup: Louie: 'You... will love me... if I never see you again'
jokes.punchline: Emily's matter-of-fact confirmation of the absurd logic
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of completely illogical romantic condition

jokes.text: You're a very complicated girl, Emily.
jokes.setup: Emily explaining her contradictory demands
jokes.punchline: Louie's massive understatement of her psychological complexity
jokes.explanation: Perfect understatement of completely insane relationship logic

jokes.text: Do we write letters?
jokes.setup: Louie about to leave Emily forever
jokes.punchline: His practical question about maintaining contact
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie moment - practical concern in romantic gesture

jokes.text: I write to you, and you never answer me.
jokes.setup: Louie asking about writing letters
jokes.punchline: Even their correspondence follows the twisted dynamic
jokes.explanation: Perfect extension of the backward relationship logic to correspondence

jokes.text: And when he does, he's going to be wearing a white coat and carrying a butterfly net.
jokes.setup: Louie saying someday Emily's Mr. Right will come along
jokes.punchline: Describing a psychiatric orderly coming to take her away
jokes.explanation: Brilliant final insult - perfect structure and character-specific vindictive wit

jokes.text: I knew where to get blueberries, but it was too far and I figured, 'The hell with it!'
jokes.setup: Louie's final parting shot at Emily
jokes.punchline: Revealing he could have gotten the blueberries but chose not to
jokes.explanation: Perfect final callback showing Louie's small rebellion and character growth
